title: Akhnoor Fort
tagline: Akhnoor Fort — heritage city of Jammu & Kashmir

overview.short: Akhnoor Fort is a fort on the right bank of the Chenab River (ancient name Asikni), 28 km from Jammu City.
overview.description: Akhnoor Fort is a fort on the right bank of the Chenab River (ancient name Asikni), 28 km from Jammu City. Construction of the fort was started by Raja Tej Singh in 1762 CE and completed by his successor Raja Dhian Singh in 1802. On 17 June 1822, Maharaja Ranjit Singh crowned Maharaja Gulab Singh at the fort's Jia Pota ghat on Chenab's riverbank. The fort has high fortification walls with bastions at regular intervals and is crowned with battlements.

topPlaces.name: Akhnoor
topPlaces.description: Akhnoor is a town and municipal committee, near city of Jammu in Jammu district of Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. It lies 28 km away from Jammu city.

topPlaces.name: Dar (village)
topPlaces.description: Dar is a small village located in the Akhnoor tehsil of the Jammu district in the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It is situated in a region with significant historical and strategic importance near the Chenab River.

topPlaces.name: Operation Grand Slam
topPlaces.description: Operation Grand Slam was a key military operation of the Indo-Pakistani War of 1965. It refers to a plan drawn up by the Pakistan Army in May 1965, that consisted of an attack on the vital Akhnoor Bridge in Jammu and Kashmir, India.

topPlaces.name: Manda, Jammu
topPlaces.description: Manda is a village and an archaeological site in the Jammu district of the Union Territory of Jammu and Kashmir, India. It was excavated by Archaeological Survey of India during 1976-77 by J. P. Joshi.

topPlaces.name: Marh
topPlaces.description: Marh is a village and municipality in Jammu district of the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir. The village is located 26 kilometres from district headquarters Jammu.

topPlaces.name: Khairi
topPlaces.description: Khairi is a village of Tehsil Mirpur, Pakistan. It is split into Upper Khairi and Lower Khairi and has the population of around 300.

topPlaces.name: Bala, Prayagraj
topPlaces.description: Bala is a village in Prayagraj, Uttar Pradesh, India.

topPlaces.name: Bhalwal, India
topPlaces.description: Bhalwal is a census town in Jammu district in the Indian union territory of Jammu and Kashmir.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Akhnoor Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Akhnoor Fort is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Akhnoor Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jammu Airport (25 km) and the nearest railway station is Katra (Vaishno Devi railhead) (22 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Akhnoor Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Akhnoor Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Akhnoor Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Akhnoor Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Akhnoor Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Akhnoor, Dar (village), Operation Grand Slam, Manda, Jammu, Marh.
